compiled_state.title: [ticket_edit]: declare -A requires bash 4+ -> fails on default macOS bash 3.2
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
Source: code review (passed) — minor finding not addressed in this session.

## Actual Behavior
ticket_edit in ticket-lib-api.sh uses bash declare -A (associative arrays) for field parsing. macOS ships bash 3.2 which does not support declare -A. The function will fail on default macOS installations.

## Expected Behavior
ticket_edit should use python3 inline for structured field parsing (consistent with sibling functions ticket_create, ticket_comment, ticket_transition which all use python3 for structured data).

## Fix
Replace the declare -A fields pattern in ticket_edit with python3-based argument parsing, consistent with the rest of ticket-lib-api.sh.
File: plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh
Category: design

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC) — declare -A bash 4+ incompatibility on macOS bash 3.2, single file, deterministic failure
comments.author: Worktree

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned — searched 3 git commits on ticket-lib-api.sh, ADR docs, and check-portability.sh. declare -A was introduced in commit 5eeec6a977 with no portability annotation. No ADR or design doc authorizes bash 4+ syntax in scripts. check-portability.sh does not catch declare -A. Sibling functions use python3 for structured parsing. This is a genuine defect.
comments.author: Worktree

comments.body: Step 3.5: hypothesis validation gate PASSED — 5 confirmed hypotheses. Step 4: auto-approved — high confidence, low risk, single file. Step 4.5: MODERATE. Step 4.75: testing_mode=GREEN — observable behavior unchanged; existing tests cover all field-update behaviors.
comments.author: Worktree

comments.body: Anti-pattern scan: 1 confirmed candidate found — plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-edit.sh:50 uses same declare -A pattern (legacy script path). Filing follow-up bug ticket.
comments.author: Worktree
